channel.title: Books on chris westland's personal website
channel.description: Recent content in Books on chris westland's personal website

item.title: Structural Equation Models
item.description: Purchase on Amazon Motivation for this Book Link to heading Since publication of the first edition of Structural Equation Models, I have been fortunate to maintain an active dialog on structural equations modeling (SEM) with many of my colleagues around the world. I never cease to be surprised with the broad divergence of opinions and myriad applications of SEM methodologies. Statistical methods such as regression and ANOVA rely on datasets of objectively measured constructs.

item.title: Audit Analytics
item.description: Purchase on Amazon I had the opportunity to share my thoughts on auditing and this book with Mike Fallat of Million Dollar Stories Podcast. Here is the link to the episode! It includes the link to Spotify and to my book on Amazon. Audit Analytics on Million Dollar Stories Please follow them on @milliondollarbookman. The 2nd edition of Audit Analytics will be out in February 2024 The crisis in accounting Link to heading Modern auditing was mandated for all publicly listed corporations after the Crash of 1929.

item.title: Financial Dynamics
item.description: Purchase on Amazon Financial Dynamics and Valuing Technology were my responses to many of the silly theorys, fads and flawed concepts surrounding e-commerce and e-commerce investment in the late 1990s. Most of this material is significantly out of date (20 years out of date). But I believe my perspective on e-commerce and the technology investment market were correct at the time, and have fared well over time.

item.title: Global Electronic Commerce
item.description: Purchase on Amazon I have studied market micro-structure since the early 1990s, and was enthused when Netscape started promoting ‘Electronic Commerce’ in the mid-1990s, offering platforms inspired by electronic securities markets (the term and concepts themselves dated from the mid-1980s and the Columbus based Compuserver). I shifted my studies to the consumer product oriented e-commerce platforms. Global Electronic Commerce was a collection of writings I had done, along with my coauthor Ted Clark, concerning our predicted evolution of e-commerce, and the particular competencies, technologies and market structures that were appropriate.

item.title: Global Innovation Management
item.description: Purchase on Amazon Motivation for this Book Link to heading This second edition of Global Innovation Management updates materials I have been using in classes taught at Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, University of Illinois – Chicago, the Vietnam EMBA and the University of Science and Technology in China. It addresses a question that I have encoun- tered for at least a decade now: ‘If you think you have invented a winning innovation, how can you bring your innovation out of the labo- ratory and turn it into a commercially success- ful product?

item.title: Valuing Technology
item.description: Purchase on Amazon Financial Dynamics and Valuing Technology were my responses to many of the silly theorys, fads and flawed concepts surrounding e-commerce and e-commerce investment in the late 1990s. Most of this material is significantly out of date (20 years out of date). But I believe my perspective on e-commerce and the technology investment market were correct at the time, and have fared well over time.

item.title: Red Wired
item.description: Purchase on Amazon I wrote this book with Sherman So, who was Technology editor at the South China Morning Post at a time China was investing heavily in the Internet. China lagged about 7 years behind the US in their infrastructure, so in 2010, their boom was just taking off and almost no one in the West was really looking at China’s Internet growth. We reviewed what we felt were the most prominent and influential Internet technology companies in China at that time, focusing on what we believed to be their core competencies.
